spec_match.rationale: Touches VideoFrame.metadata, rtpTimestamp from the chromestatus summary but skips webcodecs-video-frame-metadata-registry, dom-videoframemetadata — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, eventListener, video); controls include "Start camera"; "Stop" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5186046555586560 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on rtptimestamp-demo
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Expose rtpTimestamp from WebRTC video frames via VideoFrame.metadata() introduces webcodecs-video-frame-metadata-registry, dom-videoframemetadata but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://www.w3.org/TR/webcodecs-video-frame-metadata-registry/#dom-videoframemetadata

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on rtptimestamp-demo
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: Solid concept page for Expose rtpTimestamp from WebRTC video frames via VideoFrame.metadata(): interactive, design-system clean, and references the chromestatus entry. Tighten edge-case coverage and the spec-vocabulary surface area to take it from good to canonical.
